publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 26, 2018 at 05:42

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Norman Holmes Pearson papers consist of correspondence, writings, printed material, photographs, and audiovisual material that document a broad range of Pearson's professional and personal activities, including his research on Nathaniel Hawthorn and Ezra Pound, his management of the literary estates of H.D. and Bryher, and his activity as a professor of English and American Studies at Yale University. Documentation relating to Pearson's service in the Office of Strategic Services is partially redacted.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eleven series: I. Correspondence, 1915-1975. II. Writings, 1931-1968. III. Research Files, 1851-1978. IV. Writings of Others, 1938-1975. V. Personal Papers, 1888-1975. VI. Professional Papers, 1932-1979. VII. Photographs and Microfilm, circa 1920-1941, undated. VII. Printed Material, circa 1890-1975. VIII. Audiovisual Material, 1963-1985. IX. Realia, undated. X. Material Removed From Books, circa 1960-1975.

bioghist.head: Norman Holmes Pearson (1909-1975)
bioghist.p: Norman Holmes Pearson (Yale 1932), professor of English and American Studies at Yale University and officer in the Office of Strategic Services during the Second World War. Pearson collaborated with curator Donald Gallup to develop the Yale Collection of American Literature, playing an instrumental role in the acquisition of the papers of H.D. and Bryher.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 228 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Norman Holmes Pearson papers consist of correspondence, writings, printed material, photographs, and audiovisual material that document a broad range of Pearson's professional and personal activities, including his research on Nathaniel Hawthorne and Ezra Pound, his management of the literary estates of H.D. and Bryher, and his activity as a professor of English and American Studies at Yale University. Documentation relating to Pearson's service in the Office of Strategic Services is partially redacted.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call numbers: Za Pearson, Uncat Za Pearson.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Norman Holmes Pearson Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift and bequest of Norman Holmes Pearson, 1966-1975.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Norman Holmes Pearson Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: United States. Office of Strategic Services

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven subseries: General Correspondence, Booksellers and Publishers, Foreign Correspondence, Family Correspondence, Chronological Files, Hawthorne Edition Correspondence, and Unsorted Correspondence.

scopecontent.p: Includes typed letter from Ezra Pound to Bird, 1953 February 3.

arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically. Each chronological unit is arranged alphabetically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 228 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
